Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log Identify which cases are taking significantly longer Compute durations between first and last activity per case

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Case 2001 Submit 0900 Close 1030 1h30m Thats relatively short

Name: Backtracking - IND
Text: Case 2003 Submit 0910 Close 0930 next day 2024 04 03 0900 Wait Pay Claim 2024 04 03 0900 Close 0930

Name: Validation - PE
Text: So from submit 0910 on 04 01 to close 0930 on 04 03 correct is 48h plus 20m Thats about 2 days

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 2005 Submit 0925 04 01 Close 1430 04 04 Compute 04 01 0925 to 04 04 0925 72h then plus 5h5m 77h5m

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus cases with significant longer durations 2003 and 2005 maybe also 2002 but less

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Complexity High Also multiple requests for additional documents in 2003 and many requests in 2005

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Cases 2003 and 2005 are high complexity and both are long Cases with Low complexity are quick regardless of region

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: So root cause high complexity causing multiple document requests and delays

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource assignment Adjuster Mike and Adjuster Lisa are handling high complexity claims maybe they have higher workload or less experience causing slower evaluation and more back and forth

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region alone not the cause because Region B with low complexity 2004 is fast while Region B with high complexity 2005 is long and Region A with high complexity 2003 is also long

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Region may not be primary but perhaps resource staffing per region differs

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Thus root cause Complexity interacts with resource capacity high complexity claims require more time and multiple cycles especially when handled by a single adjuster who may be overloaded

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Implement workload balancing assign high complexity claims to senior adjusters or multiple adjusters Introduce a triage step to assess complexity early and route high complexity to specialized team Reduce document request cycles by using automated validation or presubmission checklists Provide region specific resource pools or crossregional collaboration to avoid bottlenecks Use SLA monitoring to flag cases exceeding expected duration based on complexity and resource
